channel.title: NYT > Arts > Television
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘Difficult People’ a Decade Later: A Sarcastic Time Capsule
item.description: This cult comedy about two snide show-business outsiders has a new Blu-ray edition, and a reunion this fall. The creator Julie Klausner discusses why she’d love to revive it.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Egner
item.media:description: Julie Klausner and Billy Eichner starred in “Difficult People,” which ran for three seasons on Hulu. (With Derrick Baskin, background.)

item.title: In ‘The Westies,’ Chris Brancato Expands His ‘Scumbag Universe’ (Again)
item.description: For his new series, the co-creator of “Narcos” and “Godfather of Harlem” turns his focus to a violent gang of Irish New Yorkers in the early 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Austin Considine and David Billet
item.media:credit: David Billet for The New York Times

item.title: Fans of ‘Love Island USA’ Ask: ‘Are These Couples for Real?’
item.description: For many viewers of the hit reality TV show, lively debates over whether the competing couples are truly in love is a central part of being a fan.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs

item.title: Michelle Buteau and the Surprising Transgression of “Survival of the Thickest”
item.description: The Netflix rom-com series that just ended after three seasons succeeded thanks to an irresistible performance by its star, Michelle Buteau.
item.dc:creator: Maya Phillips
item.media:description: Michelle Buteau in the third season of Netflix’s “Survival of the Thickest.”

item.title: Review: ‘Little House on the Prairie’ Adds On an Expansion
item.description: The classic story (and recent culture-war object) remains wholesome but makes room for people who called the prairie home before the Ingalls family.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik
item.category: Little House on the Prairie (2026) (TV Program)

item.title: Orna Guralnik of ‘Couples Therapy’ Considers Kendrick Lamar a Genius
item.description: Outside of her job as the therapist on the series “Couples Therapy,” she has a favorite clip of the rapper performing and shows it to everyone she knows.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times

item.title: ‘The Chosen’ Studio Is Accused of Shortchanging Crowdfunders
item.description: A lawsuit by a former shareholder says he was not fairly compensated when the company behind the popular drama about Jesus’ life went private.
item.dc:creator: Malia Mendez

item.title: ‘The Man Will Burn’: On Filming the Sacred and Propane at Burning Man
item.description: The directors of a new four-part docuseries talked about why the long-running desert festival is important — and like being on “another planet.”
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski

item.title: 2026 Emmy Nominations: ‘The Pitt’ and ‘Hacks’ Lead the Pack
item.description: The HBO Max medical series earned 25 nominations in the drama category. And in comedy, the final season of “Hacks” set a record with 24 nominations.
item.dc:creator: John Koblin

item.title: Late Night Calls Lindsey Graham’s Senate Seat ‘an Inheritance Jackpot’
item.description: “A Senate seat? Most people just get a garage full of damp boxes,” Michael Kosta said of Graham’s sister filling the late senator’s seat on “The Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: ‘The Whoopi Monologues’ Review: Expanding a One-Woman Show
item.description: Instead of one Whoopi Goldberg, audiences now experience five actresses: Kerry Washington, Kara Young, Dominique Fishback, Danielle Pinnock and Kecia Lewis.
item.dc:creator: Brittani Samuel
item.media:description: From left: Kecia Lewis, Dominique Fishback, Danielle Pinnock, Kerry Washington and Kara Young in the play “The Whoopi Monologues” at the Mitzi E. Newhouse Theater.

item.title: Jon Stewart Mocks Mitch McConnell’s Recovery Photo
item.description: The “Daily Show” host called the image “totally believable evidence that Mitch McConnell is not only alive, but the happiest boy in the hospital.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Sam Neill’s Best Movie Performances: Where to Stream
item.description: From his global breakthrough in “Jurassic Park” to the more recent “Peaky Blinders,” the actor’s range was always on display.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam

item.title: ‘Lucky’ and 6 More Shows to Watch on TV this Week
item.description: A new crime thriller comes to Apple TV. And Sean Evans hosts “Hot Ones: Extra Heat.”
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

item.title: ‘House of the Dragon’ Season 3, Episode 4 Recap: Heavy Is the Head
item.description: In her attempts to establish order, Rhaenyra is already alienating some of her most vital allies, including the Sea Snake.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins
item.media:description: Corlys (Steve Toussaint) left King’s Landing to battle pirates on the sea. It isn’t the most obvious mission for a hand of the queen to undertake.

item.title: Randolph Mantooth, Who Played John Gage on ‘Emergency!,’ Dies at 80
item.description: His portrayal of the firefighter and paramedic on the 1970s television hit helped expand the American public’s familiarity with emergency medicine.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:description: Randolph Mantooth had an extensive career as an actor on television and the stage.

item.title: ‘The Ghost in the Shell’ Director Wanted Humans in Charge
item.description: In an interview, Mokochan discusses his new adaptation of the classic cyberpunk manga and why this story continues to resonate.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Motamayor
item.category: The Ghost in the Shell (TV Program)

item.title: 5 Children’s Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month’s picks include two 2026 animated adventures and the finale to a Guillermo del Toro franchise.
item.dc:creator: Dina Gachman

item.title: Ronny Chieng Tackles Trump's Two Air Force Ones
item.description: “Is that why there’s no gas in the world anymore? Because the president flies with an extra emotional support plane?” Chieng said on “The Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Alice Oseman on the End of ‘Heartstopper’ and Her Favorite Books
item.description: While the Y.A. graphic novel phenomenon wraps up with a sixth volume and a Netflix movie, Alice Oseman mostly reads fiction about adults now.

item.title: Late Night Joins the Hunt for Mitch McConnell
item.description: “Apparently, people don’t like a majority senator disappearing with no explanation for three weeks,” Ronny Chieng said on Wednesday’s “Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Taylor Swift and Reality TV Shows Like ‘Summer House’ Earn Emmy Nods
item.description: Nominations for unscripted programs recognized a certain newlywed and the scandalous reality hit of the spring.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

item.title: Justin Bieber and Burna Boy Join Inaugural World Cup Halftime Show
item.description: The artists will perform with Madonna, Shakira and BTS in the first-ever halftime show for a World Cup final. The aim is to replicate the cultural sway of the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Morgan
item.media:description: Justin Bieber, a two-time Grammy Award winner known for hits like “Baby” and “Never Say Never,” performing at the Coachella music festival in April.

item.title: Emmys Snubs and Surprises 2026: Jon Hamm, Ariana Madix and More
item.description: Other unexpected results include nods for Chase Infiniti and the “Love Island USA” host Ariana Madix and a snub for “The Amazing Race.”
item.dc:creator: Mike Hale
item.media:credit: Jessica Kourkounis/Apple TV+, via Associated Press
item.media:description: “Your Friends & Neighbors,” the Apple TV suburban satire, was a surprise nominee for best drama, but the star, Jon Hamm, was snubbed.

item.title: ‘Love Story,’ FX’s JFK Jr. Series, Picks Up Fewer Emmy Nods Than Expected
item.description: The FX docudrama about the tragic romance between John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette received fewer nominations than its competitors.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.category: Love Story: John F Kennedy Jr & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)

item.title: Where to Stream 2026 Emmy-Nominated Series: ‘The Pitt,’ ‘Hacks’ and More
item.description: Nominees for the 78th Emmy Awards were announced on Wednesday. Here’s how to watch the shows.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.category: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms (TV Program)
Love Story: John F Kennedy Jr & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)
Only Murders in the Building (TV Program)

item.title: ‘Widow’s Bay,’ an Apple TV Breakout Hit, Scores 19 Emmy Nominations
item.description: The Apple TV horror comedy, one of the buzziest shows of the spring, has parlayed that enthusiasm into an Emmys haul, with 19 nominations.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

item.title: Why ‘Heated Rivalry’ Received No Emmy Nominations While Connor Storrie Did
item.description: Even if voters had wanted to honor the gay hockey drama, which became a hit on HBO Max, they couldn’t have. But the Emmys did recognize one of the stars.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.media:description: Connor Storrie, left, and Hudson Williams in “Heated Rivalry.” As a Canadian production, it was not eligible for Emmy nominations.

item.title: Emmy Nominees 2026: See the Full List
item.description: The 78th Emmy Awards ceremony is planned for Sept. 14. See all the nominees below.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

item.title: How ‘Little House’ Welcomes Native Neighbors
item.description: The real Ingalls family built their “little house on the prairie” on Osage land and evinced racist attitudes. A new TV adaptation tries to reckon with this while preserving the story’s heart.
item.dc:creator: Robert Ito
item.category: Little House on the Prairie (2026) (TV Program)
Little House on the Prairie (TV Program)
item.media:description: From left, Xander Cole and Corvin Mack on horseback filming a new adaptation of “Little House on the Prairie” reshaped to include Native characters and perspectives.

item.title: A New ‘Little House’ Broadens and Updates the Prairie
item.description: A Netflix adaptation of the beloved tale empowers Ma and adds new Black and Native characters to challenge prejudices. The creators know criticism is coming.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes
item.category: Little House on the Prairie (2026) (TV Program)
Little House on the Prairie (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Karen Asher for The New York Times
item.media:description: Netflix’s “Little House on the Prairie” was filmed in Winnipeg. The new Ingalls family includes Skywalker Hughes as Mary, left, and Alice Halsey as Laura.
